title: Gendo Ikari and Darth Vader
wikitext:
[]: Gendo Ikari and Darth Vader             Quote Post by synthmachine81 » Sat Nov 25, 2017 12:47 am Was just musing about this lately. Gendo Ikari is basically the Anti-Darth Vader when it comes to redemption. You know how people mock the fact that Vader is forgiven so easily it's ridiculous after all the fucked up things he's done, like slaughtering Padawans, being a ruthless commander to an authoritarian galactic empire, cutting off his own son's hand and the list goes on. Gendo also does many fucked up things, SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Gendo Ikari and Darth Vader             Quote Post by viperzero » Sat Nov 25, 2017 12:52 am

[]: Re: Gendo Ikari and Darth Vader             Quote Post by synthmachine81 » Sat Nov 25, 2017 12:56 am Hahaha love that!

[]: Re: Gendo Ikari and Darth Vader             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 25, 2017 1:16 am Gendo receives a lot of empathy from people after EoE, when it's revealed he's pretty much an adult version of Shinji with all the same fears and insecurities. As for Gendo's comparison to Vader, this is something I've actually been recently exploring for a potential fanfic. One major point of comparison is their relationship to a greater authority. Both are servants, and both have secret plans to undermine their masters. For Vader, it's Emperor Palpatine, and in Empire Strikes Back, he reveals to Luke a desire to overthrow the emperor and rule the galaxy. This suggests he's also been biding his time for a long while, waiting for an opportunity (in the comics, his plans to overthrow the Emperor develop soon after Ep IV when he learns the identity of the boy that blew up the Death Star and that Palpatine lied about his children). Meanwhile, the relationship between Gendo and Seele is much the same. Gendo is simultaneously working for and against Seele, going far enough with their plans to serve his own end, and betraying them when it matters. Both characters are cold and distant on the outside, with vulnerable hearts, and are both motivated by dead wives. Both are also masters of manipulation; Gendo goes without saying, but it should be noted how brilliantly executed Vader's plan with Cloud City and Luke went.
